pi_items.source: Task README and task.json specify exact name requirement
Sygic public documentation confirms VAN as supported vehicle type. Web search results from Sygic documentation list vehicle types including car, van, truck, bus, motorcycle, motorhome
Sygic public documentation (https://www.sygic.com/what-is/vehicle-profiles) confirms DIESEL as supported fuel type for vehicle profiles
Sygic public documentation confirms production year is a configurable vehicle profile field. 2022 is a valid year

Sygic public documentation confirms Euro emission standards 1-6 are supported. Wikipedia and other sources confirm Euro 6 is a real EU emission standard introduced in 2014 (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/European_emission_standards)

Sygic documentation confirms three route computing options: Fastest, Shortest, and Economic. However, specific numeric encoding (whether 0=Shortest, 1=Fastest, 2=Economic or another mapping) is not documented in publicly available Sygic documentation or SDK references

Sygic public documentation (https://www.sygic.com/what-is/route-computing) explicitly lists toll roads as an avoidable road type in route planning settings. false means toll roads are NOT avoided (allowed)
Task README and verifier.py confirm this is a Sygic setting. Web search results and Sygic documentation references arrive-in-direction as a supported feature

privileged_info_summary: Sygic GPS Navigation (com.sygic.aura) is a real navigation application that supports vehicle profiles with configurable attributes: vehicle types (car, van, truck, bus, motorcycle, motorhome), fuel types (including diesel), emission categories (Euro 1-6 standards), and production year. The app offers three route computing methods: fastest (least time), shortest (minimum distance), and economic (lowest fuel consumption). Route planning supports toll road avoidance and arrive-in-direction (pull-up side) features. The metadata values for vehicle_name, vehicle_type (VAN), fuel_type (DIESEL), production_year (2022), emission_category (EURO6), toll road avoidance (false=allowed), and arrive-in-direction (true) are all consistent with Sygic's documented capabilities. The specific numeric encoding for route_compute preference (0=Shortest) could not be independently verified from public documentation.
